paper_title: Using U-Net models in deep learning for brain tumor detection from MRI scans

entities.description: The primary objective of identifying the presence and location of tumors in the nervous system from MRI images.
A deep learning model architecture designed for image segmentation, particularly in the medical field.
A powerful and flexible architecture used as a backbone for image recognition and medical diagnosis.
A neural network architecture that optimizes performance and resource consumption using scaling and neural architecture search.
An architecture used for object detection at different scales by combining bottom-up and top-down feature maps.
Magnetic Resonance Imaging used to capture detailed images of brain structures.
A publicly available dataset consisting of 3,929 MRI scan images and corresponding masks for brain tumor segmentation.
The mean Intersection over Union score achieved by the hybrid EfficientNet and FPN model on the test set.
The similarity coefficient achieved by the hybrid EfficientNet and FPN model on the test set.
The Intersection over Union score achieved by the ResNeXt-50 model on the test set.
The similarity coefficient achieved by the ResNeXt-50 model on the test set.
A specialization within Artificial Intelligence used for complex image processing and detection tasks.
Author from the College of Information and Communication Technology, Can Tho University.
Author from VNPT Soc Trang, Viet Nam.
Author from the College of Information and Communication Technology, Can Tho University.
